body_txt: This character is a writer — or a novelist, playwright, screenwriter, etc. — who creates works of fiction, either simply as part of their job, or does so with a genuine passion for the craft, influencing who they are and what they do. Characterization can include putting their thoughts on what to write for their books at the forefront, usually in a manner indicating they're taking their current experience (whether their current adventure or in their daily life) as inspiration for their latest work; having a more eloquent or varied writing/speaking dialect that averts Realistic Diction Is Unrealistic , since writers are more likely to know of big and fancier words to use if they frequently write that way in their books; or even just be eccentric or disconnected from reality, especially if they've spent more time making works of fiction than anything else. More than character influence, being a writer can allow for the character to have some skill sets that benefit them for the plot, including any sort of Chekhov's Skill learned from studying for their work, or even the ability to make up scenarios to make deductions or conceivable lies. It goes without saying that many narrative works of art are created by writers, meaning that such characters may be written based on the author's experience as a writer . With that said, not all writer characters are Author Avatars or vice versa. Merely being a writer is not trope-worthy on its own, unless there's a clear influence on the character themselves. Super-Trope to Eccentric Artist . Compare with The Storyteller , who is about telling tales rather than creating them. See also Introspective Art and its sub-trope Art Reflects Personality , for when it's the work of fiction that a writer makes which says something about them, and All Art Is Autobiographical , when it's the In-Universe belief that the art reflects the creator. Has no relation to Having a Heart .
